---
title: "Version1"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# load libraries
library(readr)       
library(readxl)     
library(dplyr)   
library(mixOmics)
```
```{r}
## load in all data
# ---- Load abundance table ----
hgma_abund <- read_csv("HGMARawData/vect_atlas.csv")
# Quick check
head(hgma_abund)

# ---- Load sample metadata ----
sample_meta <- read_excel("HGMARawData/sample_metadata.xlsx")
head(sample_meta)

# ---- Load corresponding taxa table ----
corr_taxa <- read_csv("HGMARawData/corresponding_taxa.csv")
head(corr_taxa)
```


```{r}
## find sample.IDs based on study eg. PRJEB1786

study_meta <- sample_meta %>%
  dplyr::filter(BioProject == "PRJEB1786") %>%
  dplyr::select(sample.ID, Disease)          

# Quick check
head(study_meta)
```

```{r}
# ---- Normalize disease labels ----
study_meta <- study_meta %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Disease = ifelse(Disease == "NGT", "Healthy", Disease))

# ---- Filter for Healthy and T2D samples ----
disease_meta <- study_meta %>%
  dplyr::filter(Disease %in% c("Healthy", "T2D"))

# Quick check counts
table(disease_meta$Disease)
dim(disease_meta)
```

```{r}
# ---- Subset abundance table keeping taxa column ----
abund_disease <- hgma_abund %>%
  dplyr::select(taxa = 1, all_of(disease_meta$sample.ID))

# Quick check
head(abund_disease[, 1:6])  # taxa + first 5 samples
dim(abund_disease)
```
```{r}
# ---- Transpose abundance table ----
# Keep taxa as row names
abund_transposed <- abund_disease %>%
  tibble::column_to_rownames(var = "taxa") %>%  # taxa → rownames
  t() %>%                                       # transpose
  as.data.frame()                               # back to data.frame

# Quick check
head(abund_transposed[, 1:5])  # first 5 taxa columns
dim(abund_transposed)          # samples × taxa
```

```{r}
# remove unclassified
corr_taxa <- corr_taxa[!grepl("unclassified", corr_taxa$name), ]

# filter columns to those in corr_taxa$id
keep_ids <- colnames(abund_transposed) %in% corr_taxa$id
abund_transposed <- abund_transposed[, keep_ids, drop = FALSE]

# rename columns to the corresponding names
colnames(abund_transposed) <- setNames(corr_taxa$name, corr_taxa$id)[colnames(abund_transposed)]


# ---- TEST ----
head(colnames(abund_transposed))
dim(abund_transposed)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# get only subspecies with trailing space + digit
multi_taxa <- corr_taxa[grepl("\\s[0-9]$", corr_taxa$name), ]
multi_taxa$name <- sub("\\s[0-9]$", "", multi_taxa$name)

# unique species names that had subspecies
multi_taxa_lst <- unique(multi_taxa$name)

# Make a working copy
abund_species <- abund_transposed

# Function to check if main species exists
check_main_species <- function(cols) {
  nums <- as.character(1:9)
  for (col in cols) {
    last_char <- substr(col, nchar(col), nchar(col))
    if (!(last_char %in% nums)) {
      return(TRUE)
    }
  }
  return(FALSE)
}

for (taxa in multi_taxa_lst) {

  # Skip taxa with 'subtype'
  if (str_detect(tolower(taxa), "subtype")) next

  cols <- colnames(abund_species)
  alltaxa_columns <- cols[str_detect(cols, fixed(taxa))]

  if (length(alltaxa_columns) == 0) next

  if (length(alltaxa_columns) == 1) {
    abund_species[[taxa]] <- abund_species[[alltaxa_columns]]

  } else if (check_main_species(alltaxa_columns)) {
    # Main species exists → remove all subspecies columns
    subspecies_cols <- setdiff(alltaxa_columns, taxa)
    abund_species <- abund_species %>% dplyr::select(-all_of(subspecies_cols))

  } else {
    # Only subspecies exist → sum into new species column
    abund_species[[taxa]] <- rowSums(abund_species[, alltaxa_columns, drop = FALSE])
    abund_species <- abund_species %>% dplyr::select(-all_of(alltaxa_columns))
  }
}


```


```{r}

# remove columns with the same value (except disease)
abund_species <- abund_species %>%
  dplyr::select(where(~ length(unique(.x)) > 1))

dim(abund_species)

# Rename a single column
colnames(abund_species)[colnames(abund_species) == "Blautia coccoides == Blautia producta"] <- "Blautia producta"

```



```{r}
## low count removal function with cut off 0.01 %, copied from MixOmics website
low.count.removal <- function(
                        data, # OTU count df of size n (sample) x p (OTU)
                        percent=0.0001 # cutoff changed from python code
                        ) 
  {
    keep.otu = which(colSums(data)*100/(sum(colSums(data))) > percent)
    data.filter = data[,keep.otu]
    return(list(data.filter = data.filter, keep.otu = keep.otu))
}
```

```{r}
# Identify healthy and T2D samples
healthy_samples <- disease_meta$sample.ID[disease_meta$Disease == "Healthy"]
t2d_samples     <- disease_meta$sample.ID[disease_meta$Disease == "T2D"]

cat("Number of healthy samples:", length(healthy_samples), "\n")
cat("Number of T2D samples:", length(t2d_samples), "\n")

# Subset abundance table by rows
healthy_df <- abund_species[rownames(abund_species) %in% healthy_samples, , drop = FALSE]
t2d_df     <- abund_species[rownames(abund_species) %in% t2d_samples, , drop = FALSE]

cat("Number of taxa (columns) in healthy_df before low-count removal:", ncol(healthy_df), "\n")
cat("Number of taxa (columns) in t2d_df before low-count removal:", ncol(t2d_df), "\n")

# Apply low count removal
healthy_res <- low.count.removal(healthy_df, percent = 0.0001)
t2d_res     <- low.count.removal(t2d_df, percent = 0.0001)

# Extract filtered OTU tables
healthy_filtered <- healthy_res$data.filter
t2d_filtered     <- t2d_res$data.filter

cat("Number of taxa (columns) in healthy_filtered after low-count removal:", ncol(healthy_filtered), "\n")
cat("Number of taxa (columns) in t2d_filtered after low-count removal:", ncol(t2d_filtered), "\n")

# Union of OTUS
otus_to_keep <- union(colnames(healthy_filtered), colnames(t2d_filtered))
cat("Number of OTUs in union:", length(otus_to_keep), "\n")

# subset the original abundance to only keep low count union OTU columns
abund_filtered <- abund_species[, colnames(abund_species) %in% otus_to_keep, drop = FALSE]
cat("Number of taxa (columns) in abund_filtered:", ncol(abund_filtered), "\n")
```

```{r}
## get the smallest non zero number for offset
# Flatten the table to a vector, ignoring zeros
non_zero_values <- abund_filtered[abund_filtered > 0]

# Find the smallest non-zero value
min_offset <- min(non_zero_values)
```


```{r}
## apply clr for the numeric data, and add on the SID and disease afterwards. 
data.clr <- logratio.transfo(as.matrix(abund_filtered), logratio = 'CLR', offset = min_offset)
clr_mat <- unclass(data.clr) 
clr_df <- as.data.frame(clr_mat)
```

```{r}
# save data for modelling
saveRDS(clr_df, "HGMA")
```

```{r}
# --- 1. Prepare taxa tables ---
# Remove 'Disease' column (case-insensitive)
py_taxa <- py_clr[, !grepl("^disease$", colnames(py_clr), ignore.case = TRUE), drop = FALSE]
r_taxa  <- r_clr[, !grepl("^disease$", colnames(r_clr), ignore.case = TRUE), drop = FALSE]

# Remove non-numeric columns (e.g., rownames accidentally included)
py_taxa <- py_taxa[, sapply(py_taxa, is.numeric), drop = FALSE]
r_taxa  <- r_taxa[, sapply(r_taxa, is.numeric), drop = FALSE]

# Convert all columns to numeric (safe)
py_taxa[] <- lapply(py_taxa, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))
r_taxa[]  <- lapply(r_taxa, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))

# Count number of taxa
num_taxa <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("Python", "R"),
  Num_Taxa = c(ncol(py_taxa), ncol(r_taxa))
)
cat("=== Number of taxa ===\n")
print(num_taxa)

# --- 2. Column sums for matching taxa ---
common_taxa <- intersect(colnames(py_taxa), colnames(r_taxa))

col_sums_table <- data.frame(
  Taxa = common_taxa,
  Sum_Python = colSums(py_taxa[, common_taxa, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE),
  Sum_R = colSums(r_taxa[, common_taxa, drop = FALSE], na.frm = TRUE)
)
col_sums_table$Difference <- col_sums_table$Sum_Python - col_sums_table$Sum_R

cat("\n=== Column sums for matching taxa ===\n")
print(col_sums_table)

# --- 3. Row sums for each dataset (CLR should be ~0) ---
row_sums_py <- rowSums(py_taxa, na.rm = TRUE)
row_sums_r  <- rowSums(r_taxa, na.rm = TRUE)

# Quick check for near-zero row sums (floating-point errors)
row_sums_py <- round(row_sums_py, 10)
row_sums_r  <- round(row_sums_r, 10)

row_sums_table <- data.frame(
  Row = 1:nrow(py_taxa),
  Sum_Python = row_sums_py,
  Sum_R = row_sums_r
)

cat("\n=== Row sums (Python vs R) ===\n")
print(head(row_sums_table, 10))

```
